confirmed.note: CsvRenderer::Render copy-pasted to TabsRenderer::Render — identical header-loop + row-loop, only ',' -> '\t' and CsvEscape() -> raw value differ
JsonRenderer per-row object-emitting body copied verbatim into JsonLinesRenderer; only the outer wrapper ([\n ... ]) vs newline-per-row differs — whole null/numeric/bool/string branch duplicated

GetDynamicNumericFunctions/StringFunctions/SystemFunctions/DateTimeFunctions are clone-stamped: call ExecuteSqlAndGetStringVector(<sql>) then identical 'if(result.ok()) return ValueOrDie(); return {};' tail; only the SQL string literal changes
Single-quote string-state block copy-pasted to double-quote identifier block: same toggle-flag/set_color/++i/continue skeleton, flag renamed in_single_quote->in_double_quote and color YELLOW->DEFAULT

false_positives.what_happened: Five small escape() helpers share the canonical character-substitution idiom: take string, loop chars, append replacement-or-char, return. Token streams collide heavily (loop header, append calls, return) even though the escape MAPS are entirely different (CSV quote-doubling vs JSON \u vs HTML & vs LaTeX backslash). Detector sees structural twins; semantically they are distinct, deliberate, and irreducible — merging them would need a table-driven escaper that hurts readability.
false_positives.fix_proposal: #056: for short functions (< ~15 tokens of body) weight STRING/CHAR literal divergence much higher in the similarity score — when the set of string/char literals between two candidate blocks is disjoint, cap similarity (these are 'same skeleton, different data' which is the escaper/dispatch idiom). Equivalent to a literal-aware penalty already used for data-tables, extended to the small-function regime.

false_positives.what_happened: gizmosql_security.cpp is a renamed fork of the upstream Arrow Flight SQL example flight_sql_security.cpp (SecurityUtilities::FlightServerTlsCertificates, FindKeyValPrefixInCallHeaders, CreateJWTToken, etc. all carry the ASF license header). This commit only reflows '&'/'*' spacing and adds logging; the cross-file similarity the detector reports is a pre-existing vendored/forked whole file, not authored copy-paste, and was already attributed in round1 (d7fdcf3).
false_positives.fix_proposal: #056: treat pairs that share the verbatim ASF/Apache license header AND have a sibling original under a vendored/example path (here the parallel src/library/flight_sql_* tree) as forked-file, not chunk copy — exclude from per-chunk reporting (whole-file vendor/fork class), same handling as protobuf/moc headers.

cycle.desc: no include cycle — archcheck --graph reports sccs_cyclic=0, largest_scc=1 over 125 nodes / 263 edges
cycle.evidence: graph clean; src/ is layered (client/ -> common/ -> duckdb|sqlite backends) with no back-edges; 113 unresolved + 731 external are vendored Arrow/DuckDB headers, not authored nodes
